title: Consecutive Order Streaks
description: Identify customers who have placed orders on at least three consecutive days.
sql_solution: WITH t AS (SELECT customer_id, date, julianday(date) - ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY date) grp FROM orders) SELECT DISTINCT customer_id FROM t GROUP BY customer_id, grp HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3;
pyspark_solution:
from pyspark.sql.functions import *
from pyspark.sql.window import Window

sequence_window = Window.partitionBy("customer_id").orderBy(to_date(col("date")))

streaks = orders.withColumn(
    "grp",
    datediff(to_date(col("date")), lit("1970-01-01").cast("date")) - row_number().over(sequence_window)
)

result = (
    streaks.groupBy("customer_id", "grp")
    .agg(count(lit(1)).alias("streak_length"))
    .filter(col("streak_length") >= 3)
    .select("customer_id")
    .distinct()
)
